
\color{blue}

**Solution**. C.\

`get_random_normal` generates an array of dimension `shape`.
JAX `jit` has to know the memory allocation at compile time.
If `shape` is `static` then `jit` will compile the function treating `shape` as fixed.
JAX will reuse this compiled code unless the function is called with a previously unseen value of `shape` that triggers a recompilation.

The memory requirements do not depend on the value of `key`, so it is fine to include this as a variable at compilation time.

The other suggestions are false. For (E), the `@jit` decorator is a convenient shortcut but is exactly equivalent to a call to `jit`

\color{black}
